raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX pm, while on vacation in XXXX XXXX in the XXXX XXXX, I received a text message from XXXX Bank stating that a purchase in the amount of {$2400.00} at XXXX XXXX had been declined pending verification. I immediately called the bank and reported the transaction as fraudulent. They cancelled my card and I destroyed the card. I then checked the other XXXX credit cards that I had with me and found that both had similar charges posted to them for the same merchant. ( XXXX XXXX Bank ( XXXX Card ) in the amount of {$1400.00} and Barclay 's Bank ( XXXX Rewards card ) in the amount of {$2600.00}. I immediately contacted each bank and reported the transactions as fraudulent and was told by both of them to destroy the cards. Several weeks later, I received notification from XXXX XXXX that the transaction had been reposted because it was a " chip '' transaction. I called and disputed the transaction again and received notification that the transaction was indeed fraudulent and the transaction was reversed on XX/XX/XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, I received notification that the minimum payment was due on my XXXX 's account. The amount was quite a bit larger than the normal minimum payment and when I checked my account found that they had reposted the {$2600.00} charge on XX/XX/XXXX. I called to dispute the charge again. They said that it was determined that this was not a fraudulent charge because the chip card was used and I said that the card had not left my possession. When I researched the merchant, they are located in XXXX XXXX which is hours away from XXXX XXXX and we never left the resort. Barclays said I needed to submit a written request. A letter including back up documentation from all the banks involved was sent via certified mail on XX/XX/XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a voicemail from Barclay 's Bank that the paperwork had been received and they were reaching back out to the investigations team. On XX/XX/XXXX I received a call saying there was no fraud. I again disputed the charge and was told they would resubmit to investigations. On XX/XX/XXXX they called again and denied the fraud and said they would not investigate any further. At no time did I initiate any of these transactions, nor did I receive any services for this charge.
